action.hypothesis: Recent gated LR sweeps regressed, which suggests the quality ceiling is now architectural rather than a simple optimizer bracket. A new routed SAE family that separates top-k selection scores from decoded magnitudes, while warm-starting the support branch from the magnitude encoder, may preserve useful medium-strength features that plain gated currently suppresses too early and thus reduce FVU at K=128.
action.summary: Prototype a new `routed` SAE family and run it in proxy at K=128 using the current strongest gated training regime as the comparison anchor.
action.self_review: This is one coherent architecture bet: add a launcher-compatible family under `sparsify/` and evaluate it with otherwise fixed training settings. It responds directly to the stalled gated LR search without drifting into multi-axis tuning. The main risk is that the extra routing branch may not beat gated immediately, but proxy is the right incubation tier for that uncertainty.
notes_to_memory: Added a new `routed` SAE family that uses support-aware top-k scores but decodes with gated magnitudes, so routing and coefficient shrinkage are no longer the same operation.
The routed family warm-starts its gate projection from the magnitude encoder instead of an all-zero gate branch, aiming to reduce optimization drag seen in later gated stabilization sweeps.
A local forward/backward sanity check passed for the new architecture before launching training.
next_hypotheses: If routed proxy FVU beats gated proxy materially, promote it and treat routed-specific stabilization as the new main quality path.
If routed quality is competitive but throughput regresses, keep the family alive and follow with a performance-focused implementation pass rather than discarding it on speed alone.
If routed is clearly worse, conclude that gated-family architectural refinement needs a different mechanism and try a different new family instead of more LR-only sweeps.
